evals.name: branch-full-review
evals.prompt: do a full code review of my branch against main
evals.expected_output: Detects branch mode, selects all 6 review types, launches parallel subagents, runs synthesis, delivers consolidated report
expectations: Scope mode is detected as 'branch' (comparing current branch against a base branch)
Changed files list is present in scope-determination.md showing files that differ between master and main
All 6 review types are selected: standard, security, performance, architecture, test-coverage, and silent-failure
Subagent launch was attempted for each of the 6 selected review types
A final report file (final-report.md) exists with review findings or a synthesis output

evals.name: uncommitted-subset
evals.prompt: review my local changes, just standard and security
evals.expected_output: Detects uncommitted mode, selects only standard + security review types, launches 2 subagents, runs synthesis, delivers consolidated report
expectations: Scope mode is detected as 'uncommitted' (reviewing local staged/unstaged changes, not branch comparison or PR)
Only standard and security review types are selected — NOT performance, architecture, test-coverage, or silent-failure
No more than 2 review subagents were launched (one for standard, one for security)
A final report file (final-report.md) exists with review findings

evals.name: file-comprehensive
evals.prompt: comprehensive review of the skills in .claude/plugins/code-steward-reviews/skills/standard-review/ and .claude/plugins/code-steward-reviews/skills/security-review/
evals.expected_output: Detects file mode (ad-hoc review, no diff), selects all 6 review types, launches parallel subagents reviewing those specific directories, delivers consolidated report
expectations: Scope mode is detected as 'file' (ad-hoc codebase review of specific directories, no diff scoping)
Scope references the two specific skill directories (standard-review and security-review)
All 6 review types are selected (user said 'comprehensive')
A final report file (final-report.md) exists with review findings
